reason: data/politics.db is unreadable from the Linux FUSE sandbox — sqlite returns 'unable to open database file' on direct open and 'database disk image is malformed' when copied. Same intermittent FUSE-mount issue noted in project memory. The DB file is fine on Windows; the issue is the mount layer's compatibility with sqlite's WAL/locking. This brief requires reading politician_events joined to events — cannot be processed Linux-side.
recommendation: Run this brief from the Windows side, OR refactor the brief generator to embed the necessary politician_events rows into the brief context.events_payload at queue time so the Linux drain doesn't need DB access. The second approach is preferable for reliability — the AI side should not assume DB access since it's intermittent.
